test_cases.input: Article: Profile of a surgeon who left a high-paying hospital job to practice in a rural area for a fraction of the pay. Headline: 'The Doctor Who Chose Somewhere Else'. Newsletter tone: Literary, personal essays and narrative journalism audience.
test_cases.output_summary: Generated 3 subject lines including 'The surgeon who walked away' (curiosity), 'He left a $500k job for a town of 3,000' (direct), and 'Why a surgeon gave it all up' (question frame). Preview text: 'A choice that makes sense once you know the whole story.' Teaser body 95 words, correctly withheld the payoff, opened with a specific scene from the piece. Tone matched the literary register throughout. Link cue clean.

test_cases.input: Article: Data investigation showing which cities have the highest proportion of empty office buildings post-pandemic, and what municipalities plan to do with them. Headline: 'Dead Floors'. Newsletter tone: Punchy, policy-adjacent, urban affairs audience.
test_cases.output_summary: Generated 3 subject lines including 'The empty floors problem' (direct), 'What happens to a city with 30% office vacancy?' (question), and 'Downtown is emptying out. Cities are figuring it out.' (descriptive). Preview text under 80 characters. Teaser body correctly led with a specific statistic from the article rather than a generic setup. Punchy tone maintained. One subject line was 58 characters — slightly over optimal but acceptable.

test_cases.input: Article: Review and buying guide for consumer noise-cancelling headphones. Newsletter tone: Consumer tech, casual, product-focused audience. Paywall note requested.
test_cases.output_summary: Generated 3 subject lines appropriate for consumer tech register. Teaser body correctly framed as a recommendation guide without giving away the winner. Paywall note added in teaser body as instructed. Link cue clean. Tone was appropriately casual and consumer-friendly. One subject line slightly generic for a competitive tech newsletter space — could have been more specific about what differentiates this guide.
